verdict_reasoning: FN1 (fibronectin) is a secreted glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. It is secreted as a soluble dimer and reaches the cell surface by binding cell-surface integrins (e.g., α5β1) and ECM components — a reversible, equilibrium non-covalent interaction that fails the recruitment/wash test. The integrin is the surface target; FN1 is the recruited protein. ECM deposition (including oncology-targeted EDB+FN ADC programs) is explicitly stromal/matrix deposition, not stable surface anchoring. Pre-no contextual checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no gene-specific experimental evidence of outer-leaflet PM display in cancer/apoptosis/activation states beyond integrin binding; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — FN1 has no membrane anchor of its own in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM pool via its own anchor; (5) stable_surface_attachment — integrin binding is a reversible equilibrium, not wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent TM-partner tethering.
